title: My Intuition Is Allowed to Be Reliable
subtitle: An affirmation for trusting the signal you have been second-guessing
excerpt: A short affirmation for the pattern of second-guessing an inner signal that has, in fact, been right before. Affirms that the intuition is a legitimate source of information, not a noise to be managed.

content.text: A short affirmation for the pattern of dismissing an inner signal as irrational, paranoid, or wishful thinking when it has, on reflection, been right before. The affirmation is not a claim that intuition is infallible. It is a claim that it is worth listening to.
Say it in the morning, three times. Say it again when the second-guessing starts.
My intuition is allowed to be reliable. I listen to it.

The first sentence is permission: the signal is allowed to be a signal, not just noise. The second is an instruction you give yourself. Both are present tense. Neither claims certainty about what the intuition is pointing at, only that it is worth hearing.
